topic: Study optimal saturation design for two-stage randomized-saturation experiments under isolated partial interference by making the full bounded-support moment program the theorem object: under a specialized within-cluster homogeneous linear-in-share working model (a specialization of Cai, Pouget-Abadie and Airoldi 2022, not the full parent model), minimize V(nu)=V0+V1*m2+V3*m3+V4*(m4-m2^2) over laws nu on [0,1] with mean pbar. Prove the main result as a necessary-and-sufficient quartic dual/KKT global-optimality certificate for any candidate law, with a support-at-most-3 optimizer reduction. Only as a scoped corollary, in the positive-penalty symmetric-candidate regime V1>0,V4>0, characterize when the stratified symmetric law delta_pbar is globally optimal by the closed threshold min_{d in [-pbar,1-pbar]}(V1+V3*d+V4*d^2)>=0; when this fails construct the skewed two/three-point optimizer. Include the witness pbar=1/3,V1=1,V4=1,V3=-10: (2/3)delta_0+(1/3)delta_1 has m2=2/9,m3=2/27,m4-m2^2=2/81 and improves by -40/81 over delta_pbar. Recover the SUTVA corner V3=V4=0: V is linear in m2, so V1>0 gives within-cluster stratified delta_pbar, V1<0 gives cluster Bernoulli (1-pbar)delta_0+pbar delta_1, and V1=0 ties. Add finite-M attainability for denominator-compatible support masses/saturations and an O(1/M+1/m) rounding-loss clause, distinguishing M*pbar integrality for cluster Bernoulli vertices from m*pbar integrality for within-cluster stratified saturation.

open_problems.open_problem: Give a necessary-and-sufficient global optimality certificate for the full bounded-support randomized-saturation moment program with objective V0+V1*m2+V3*m3+V4*(m4-m2^2).

source_refs.claim: Cai, Pouget-Abadie, and Airoldi show that cluster-based or stratified randomized saturation designs are optimal under SUTVA, that this no longer holds under interference, and that their paper investigates optimization after deriving closed-form bias and variance.
source_refs.author: Chencheng Cai, Jean Pouget-Abadie, Edoardo M. Airoldi

source_refs.claim: Baird, Bohren, McIntosh, and Ozler formalize randomized-saturation designs and derive power tradeoffs over finite saturation sets, but not a global certificate over all bounded-support laws.
source_refs.author: Sarah Baird, J. Aislinn Bohren, Craig McIntosh, Berk Ozler

source_refs.excerpt: Give a necessary-and-sufficient bounded-support KKT certificate
Replace the Theorem 1 certificate with a true global certificate
omits the non-linear residual from the -V_4 m_2(nu)^2 term

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published randomized-saturation literature supplies the two-stage design framework and variance algebra, but it stops short of certifying global optimality over arbitrary laws on [0,1] with fixed mean. The archived proposal tried to fill exactly this gap, but reviewers found its certificate only first-order/local because it dropped the nonlinear -V4*m2(nu)^2 residual; the unresolved obstacle is turning the compact but nonconvex moment objective into a dual/KKT statement that is both necessary and sufficient.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A resolution would be a quartic dual polynomial/KKT theorem: for any candidate law nu on [0,1] with mean pbar, nonnegativity on the interval, contact on support, and complementary slackness certify global optimality, with a support-at-most-3 optimizer reduction.

writing_exemplar.why: Its framing already moves from randomized-saturation designs under SUTVA to the fact that interference breaks endpoint optimality.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: randomized saturation nests stratified and cluster designs -> SUTVA gives endpoint optimality -> interference breaks that endpoint result -> certify the exact optimum in a specialized homogeneous linear-in-share moment program

method_exemplars.technique: Carry over the closed-form bias/variance expansion under linear interference as the source of the V0,V1,V3,V4 moment objective.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The resolution's theorem object is a specialization of the Cai-Pouget-Abadie-Airoldi randomized-saturation variance calculation.

method_exemplars.technique: Use extreme-point geometry for moment sets after profiling the nonlinear m2 term to justify finite-atomic optimizer reduction.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The bounded-support law optimization becomes a moment-set problem once the nonlinear residual is handled rather than ignored.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize exactly when the within-cluster stratified law delta_pbar is globally optimal in the positive-penalty symmetric-candidate regime, and construct the skewed optimizer when it is not.

source_refs.claim: The paper emphasizes that under interference optimal randomized-saturation designs need not be cluster-based or stratified, but it does not isolate this closed skew-threshold corollary.
source_refs.author: Chencheng Cai, Jean Pouget-Abadie, Edoardo M. Airoldi

source_refs.claim: Offer-Westort and Dimmery show that two-stage randomization with treatment correlation strictly between zero and one can dominate one-stage designs for homogeneous-policy estimands, reinforcing that endpoints are not enough.
source_refs.author: Molly Offer-Westort, Drew Dimmery

source_refs.excerpt: restrict to the parameter domain where the symmetric benchmark is actually optimal at V3=0
Repair the V3 = 0 benchmark by carrying the Bernoulli vertex's V4 variance term

open_problems.why_unsolved: Existing papers and the prior CausalSmith archive agree that interference can make endpoint or stratified benchmarks fail, but earlier attempts overclaimed a universal signed threshold and were algebraically wrong off the narrow positive-penalty branch. The missing piece is a branch-correct threshold that is explicitly scoped to V1>0,V4>0 and tied to an actual optimizer construction when the inequality fails.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A resolution would prove that, for V1>0 and V4>0, delta_pbar is globally optimal if and only if min_{d in [-pbar,1-pbar]}(V1+V3*d+V4*d^2)>=0, and otherwise derive the skewed two- or three-point optimizer including the pbar=1/3,V1=1,V4=1,V3=-10 strict-improvement witness.

writing_exemplar.why: It provides the closest narrative for why SUTVA benchmark designs stop being reliable under interference.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: SUTVA endpoint rules are familiar -> linear interference adds saturation-moment skew terms -> stratification can cease to be optimal -> a closed inequality should decide the positive-penalty branch

method_exemplars.technique: Reuse the saturation variance algebra and compare candidate laws through their second, third, and fourth moment terms.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The threshold is a sharp comparison inside the same randomized-saturation variance objective.

method_exemplars.technique: Reduce failed-threshold cases to finite-support moment optimizers and certify equality/inequality by a supporting polynomial.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The skewed optimizer is a finite-atomic extremal law over a bounded moment class.

method_exemplars.technique: Mirror the design-tradeoff comparison style: show a concrete saturation law improves the variance objective relative to the benchmark.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The witness is only useful downstream if it is framed as a deployable randomized-saturation design comparison.

open_problems.open_problem: Convert an optimal continuous saturation law into an exact or rounded finite two-stage randomized-saturation design while separating cluster-count and within-cluster denominator constraints.

source_refs.claim: Cai, Pouget-Abadie, and Airoldi define integer treated counts through floor(pi_j N_j) and assume clusters are large enough to ignore flooring, leaving finite denominator effects outside the main theorem.
source_refs.author: Chencheng Cai, Jean Pouget-Abadie, Edoardo M. Airoldi

source_refs.claim: Basse and Feller highlight practical complications in two-stage experiments, including unequal cluster sizes and the distinction between household-weighted and individual-weighted estimands.
source_refs.author: Guillaume Basse, Avi Feller

source_refs.excerpt: Convert the continuous bounded-support saturation optimizer into an exact finite-M
Split finite implementation compatibility into M pbar integrality
explicit floor/ceiling split construction that can add at most two nearby grid supports
delta_pbar with N pbar integral but m pbar nonintegral cannot be rounded

open_problems.why_unsolved: Continuous saturation-law optimization hides two different arithmetical constraints: support masses are implemented by the number of clusters M, while saturation points are implemented by within-cluster treated counts on a 1/m grid. The archived versions mixed these conditions, and the published literature either assumes large clusters or analyzes finite designs without an optimizer-preserving rounding-loss certificate.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A resolution would prove exact finite-M attainability when support masses and saturation points are denominator-compatible, plus an O(1/M+1/m) rounding-loss bound that distinguishes M*pbar integrality for cluster Bernoulli vertices from m*pbar integrality for within-cluster stratified delta_pbar.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the closest practical two-stage-experiment paper for explaining why finite implementation details matter.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: two-stage randomization is attractive under partial interference -> implementation uses finite cluster and within-cluster counts -> continuous optimal laws may not be exactly attainable -> denominator-compatible realization and rounding loss are needed

method_exemplars.technique: Use their finite two-stage design notation and variance accounting as the implementation-level language for exact and rounded designs.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The open problem is not only an abstract law result; it must produce an actual finite randomized experiment.

method_exemplars.technique: Compare rounded laws through perturbations of m2,m3,m4 and the resulting variance objective.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The rounding-loss clause is measured in the same moment objective as the main theorem.

open_problems.open_problem: Show that the skew-aware saturation moment program recovers the no-interference SUTVA simplex-vertex rule as an exact degenerate corner, including the correct finite integrality clauses.

source_refs.claim: Under SUTVA, Cai, Pouget-Abadie, and Airoldi prove that either the cluster-based or stratified randomized-saturation design is optimal for the difference-in-means estimator.
source_refs.author: Chencheng Cai, Jean Pouget-Abadie, Edoardo M. Airoldi

source_refs.excerpt: recovers the no-interference SUTVA simplex-vertex rule
finite stratified rule requires m pbar to be integral

open_problems.why_unsolved: The endpoint comparison is already known as a standalone SUTVA result, so it is not a novelty kernel by itself. The unresolved need for this run is a noncircular reduction from the same quartic certificate to the linear m2 corner, with no mismatch between the cluster Bernoulli integrality condition and the within-cluster stratified condition.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A resolution would prove from the same certificate that when V3=V4=0 the objective is linear in m2: V1>0 gives delta_pbar, V1<0 gives (1-pbar)delta_0+pbar delta_1, and V1=0 ties, with finite attainability clauses using m*pbar for delta_pbar and M*pbar for the cluster Bernoulli vertex.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the source of the no-interference endpoint benchmark that the new theorem must recover.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: randomized saturation contains stratified and cluster designs -> SUTVA endpoint optimality is known -> an interference theorem must collapse to that benchmark -> prove the exact V3=V4=0 degeneration

method_exemplars.technique: Use the SUTVA variance comparison as the boundary condition for the moment-program theorem.
method_exemplars.why_closest: This is the exact prior-art result the proposed certificate must reproduce, not merely cite.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine which departures from isolated homogeneous within-cluster linear-in-share interference preserve a finite-dimensional saturation-moment optimal-design certificate.

source_refs.claim: Lu, Shi, and Ding state that randomized-saturation work has focused on within-cluster spillovers while practical settings may have between-cluster spillovers.
source_refs.author: Sizhu Lu, Lei Shi, Peng Ding

source_refs.claim: Leung argues exact neighborhood-interference restrictions are violated in common social-interaction models and develops approximate neighborhood interference.
source_refs.author: Michael P. Leung

source_refs.claim: Cai, Zhang, and Airoldi note that treatment assignments and interference levels are entangled on connected networks, motivating independent-set experimental designs.
source_refs.author: Chencheng Cai, Xu Zhang, Edoardo M. Airoldi

open_problems.why_unsolved: The main topic is intentionally scoped to isolated partial interference and a homogeneous linear-in-share specialization. Recent work moves toward between-cluster spillovers, approximate network interference, and graph-based designs, where the variance objective may depend on cross-cluster moments, exposure propensities, graph summaries, or compliance terms rather than only m2,m3,m4 of a saturation law.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A resolution would be a classification theorem or counterexample pair identifying the weakest extension of the isolated homogeneous model that still reduces optimal design to a finite-dimensional moment program, and showing which natural extensions necessarily require extra cross-moments or graph-dependent state variables.

writing_exemplar.why: It gives the cleanest contrast between within-cluster saturation designs and the frontier with between-cluster spillovers.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: randomized saturation is useful under partial interference -> practical settings may include spillovers across clusters -> homogeneous within-cluster moment reductions may fail -> classify when a finite-dimensional design certificate survives

method_exemplars.technique: Use approximate-interference arguments to expose which exact cutoff assumptions are doing work in a reduced design theorem.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The proposed classification turns on whether relaxing isolated interference keeps or destroys the low-dimensional moment representation.

method_exemplars.technique: Use independent-set design logic as an adjacent way to control exposure entanglement when saturation moments alone are insufficient.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is a structurally analogous design response to the failure of simple saturation randomization under connected-network interference.

literature_map:
Randomized-saturation designs begin from two-stage partial-interference setups such as Hudgens-Halloran 2008 and the econometric design calculations of Baird-Bohren-McIntosh-Ozler 2017.
Baird et al. map partial interference into clustered-error power calculations, derive standard-error tradeoffs over finite saturation supports, and provide design software.
Basse-Feller 2016 focuses on analyzing two-stage experiments in practice, especially finite cluster-size complications, weighting, variance, regression equivalence, and randomization inference.
Cai-Pouget-Abadie-Airoldi 2022 is the closest anchor: randomized saturation nests stratified and cluster designs; under SUTVA one endpoint is optimal; under interference this no longer holds.
That paper derives closed-form bias and variance under a linear interference model and investigates optimization, but it does not state a necessary-and-sufficient quartic certificate over all bounded-support laws with fixed mean.
Offer-Westort-Dimmery 2021 and Han-Owusu-Shin 2026 show saturation can also be a policy or ranking object, creating a tension between variance-optimal design and welfare-learning design.
Viviano 2026 optimizes network experiments using pilot-based variance/regret guarantees, an adjacent design route rather than a closed-form partial-interference moment program.
Lu-Shi-Ding 2026 pushes randomized saturation toward between-cluster spillovers, while Leung 2021 studies approximate neighborhood interference and Cai-Zhang-Airoldi 2023 uses independent-set design under connected-network interference.
These extension papers are useful scope guards: the proposed gap should be framed as a specialized isolated homogeneous within-cluster theorem, not as a solution to full network interference.
The live narrow niche is therefore a complete bounded-support moment theorem for the Cai-style homogeneous saturation objective, plus a sharp positive-penalty skew threshold, finite implementation clauses, and SUTVA-corner recovery.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined the direct archived qid exp_saturation_skew_threshold under CausalSmith/doc/formalization/_restart_archive/exp_saturation_skew_threshold_FAILED_proposal_1782548511, especially discovery/gaps.json and reviewer JSONs angle0_v1, angle0_v2, angle0_v3, and angle1_v1.
The archive surfaced the same core gap: a global quartic/KKT certificate, but reviewers flagged that the old certificate omitted the nonlinear -V4*m2(nu)^2 residual.
Reviewer strengthening_paths also prescribed branch repair for the symmetric threshold, including restriction to a parameter domain where the symmetric benchmark is actually optimal at V3=0.
The finite-implementation trail repeatedly distinguishes M*pbar compatibility for cluster vertices from m*pbar compatibility for within-cluster stratified saturation; angle1_v1 adds the floor/ceiling split issue.
The skewed two/three-point witness appears in archived gaps.json and was marked new relative to Cai-Pouget-Abadie-Airoldi, while adjacent network-design bank entries warned against infeasible numerical witnesses.
The support-at-most-3 reduction was not emitted as its own open problem because reviewers linked it to standard moment-set extreme-point theory; it is kept as supporting machinery for the global certificate.
Study-pipeline LATE/Manski/interval-regression gaps were reviewed as additional candidates but were not retained because they are topic-mismatched for this randomized-saturation experimentation anchor.
